danger_signals.visual_form: Approaching planes during helicopter missions requiring identification via radio communication
danger_signals.meaning: Threat requiring player to hail and determine friend-or-foe status; incorrect response triggers firefight

danger_signals.visual_form: Guard behavior change indicating awareness of player deception
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent combat or detection; player must use gas canister or gas grenades before guards alert facility

danger_signals.visual_form: Hidden environmental hazards detectable only via mine detector equipment
danger_signals.meaning: Instant death hazard requiring specialized detection tool to navigate safely

danger_signals.visual_form: Barriers blocking access to mission objectives
danger_signals.meaning: Requires security card acquisition; without card, mission progression impossible

danger_signals.visual_form: 10-minute timer (20 real-time minutes) displayed during sixth mission
danger_signals.meaning: Time-pressure danger; failure to escape within window results in mission failure
Landing without whisper mode (silent approach) reveals player position to base defenses

safety_signals.visual_form: Guards accepting forged documents without suspicion
safety_signals.meaning: Player identity as enemy force member accepted; safe passage through area

safety_signals.visual_form: Helicopter entering silent approach mode during landing
safety_signals.meaning: Undetected infiltration possible; reduces enemy alert status

safety_signals.visual_form: Temporary invisibility state applied to rescued scientist (Dr. Phineas Gump)
safety_signals.meaning: Safe escape possible; enemy cannot detect invisible unit

safety_signals.visual_form: Successful helicopter return flight with fewer enemy encounters than outbound
safety_signals.meaning: Mission completion imminent; reduced threat environment on return trajectory

safety_signals.visual_form: Card discovery in cabinet within multi-room buildings
safety_signals.meaning: Access to restricted areas granted; mission progression enabled

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Johnny 'Jimbo Baby' McGibbits hero identity; Gizmo DHX-3 helicopter as faction asset
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Home base (return destination); friendly aircraft responding to radio hails

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mad leader as antagonist authority; hostile aircraft and ground guards as faction enforcers
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinguishable via radio communication failure or hostile response; guards visually distinct from player
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemy bases with multi-room compounds; security doors; missile control rooms; nerve gas facilities; chemical containers; guard patrols

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Unidentified planes requiring friend-or-foe determination via ADF and radio hailing
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Visual distinction from confirmed friendly or hostile based on player radio interaction outcome
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Airspace around infiltration zones; encountered during helicopter transit missions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: ADF (Automatic Direction Finder) programming; helicopter equipment status terminal displaying available tools
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player configures course and verifies helicopter systems before takeoff; mission-specific inventory loaded

world_state_to_visual.world_state: En route to enemy base (helicopter phase)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Approaching aircraft requiring identification; enemy planes in airspace; radar/radio communication interface
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must hail approaching aircraft, determine threat status, engage in combat if identification fails or enemy confirmed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Helicopter descending toward landing zone; enemy base visible; detection risk high without whisper mode
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Whisper mode activation required to avoid detection; safe landing enables ground mission transition

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multi-room compound buildings; guard NPCs; cabinet-stored security cards; locked doors
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Guards respond to player papers (deception tool); suspicion triggers require gas canister/grenade use; security cards unlock progression paths

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Chemical containers for analysis; captured scientist (Dr. Gump) waiting rescue; missile control rooms for sabotage; nerve gas vat for neutralization
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mission 2: analyze containers to identify neutralizer, apply to nerve gas vat. Mission 4: rescue scientist, apply invisibility pill for escape. Mission 6: plant explosives in all missile rooms before timer expires

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Return path with reduced enemy aircraft; helicopter flight back to home base
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Fewer combat encounters on return; mission completion triggers four-letter passcode for future replay

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Guard detection leading to combat; mine detonation; timer expiration in final mission; unsuccessful helicopter landing
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mission restart required; passcode system allows return to specific mission without replaying earlier objectives

environmental_storytelling.example: Papers, gas canister, gas grenades, mine detector, explosives communicate player infiltration methodology; different equipment enables different narrative approaches (deception vs. incapacitation vs. sabotage)

Chemical containers hidden in compound rooms; nerve gas vat requiring neutralizer; missile control rooms scattered throughout bases; Dr. Gump prisoner rescue point—each location tells story of enemy operations

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: ADF navigation, Status terminal, Turbo engine, Whisper mode, Radio Communications, missiles, guns, chaffs, flares
environmental_storytelling.example: Advanced helicopter capabilities suggest technological superiority of player faction; whisper mode vs. loud operation creates stealth-vs-direct tension
Hailing unknown aircraft reveals intent and allegiance; successful radio response indicates friend; no response or hostile reply indicates enemy—communication itself becomes world-state indicator
10-minute countdown in final mission (20 real-time minutes) creates story beat of escalating threat; missile base chain reaction suggests catastrophic consequences for delay

environmental_storytelling.technique: Multi-phase mission structure as escalating stakes
environmental_storytelling.example: Six missions paired as helicopter transit + ground infiltration creates narrative of progressive penetration into enemy infrastructure; later missions introduce more complex objectives (rescue, multi-location sabotage)

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Flies toward player aircraft; requires radio hail to determine allegiance; hostile aircraft attack if player fires first or hailing fails
Engages in firefight with player helicopter; defeated via missiles/guns; player must manage ammo and flare/chaff countermeasures
Passes player without engagement; allows safe passage through airspace
Patrol compound; accept forged papers without challenge; do not attack player
Question player identity; papers deception begins to fail; require incapacitation via gas canister or gas grenades before alert is sounded
Attack player; mission compromised; area becomes unsafe for stealth infiltration
Waits in designated location; cannot move independently; requires player to provide invisibility pill for escape protection
Becomes undetectable to enemies; player can safely extract from enemy base without guard interference

notes: Infiltrator is fundamentally a deception-based world where visual cues communicate threat levels through guard behavior and aircraft identification systems. The game's world-state manifestation relies heavily on non-visual communication (radio hailing, papers deception) paired with behavioral tells (guard suspicion, aircraft response). The NES version cited here is actually Infiltrator II rebranded, suggesting the manifestation systems may differ from original 1986 computer versions. Limited visual/behavioral documentation available; inferences drawn from gameplay mechanics described in source material.
